off.label: Day Off
off.blurb: Rest day. Take it. Recovery is part of the plan.

recovery.label: Recovery
recovery.blurb: Light mobility work to keep you moving and aid recovery.

uppers.label: Uppers
uppers.blurb: Upper body + core. Push the exercises to make progress.

single.label: Single Session
single.blurb: One quality session — carry it out well.

double.label: Double Session
double.blurb: The toughest days. High effort, then recover well.

rpeScale.label: Light Activity
rpeScale.note: Can maintain for hours, easy to breathe and hold a conversation

rpeScale.label: Moderate Activity
rpeScale.note: Can exercise for long periods, able to talk in short conversations

rpeScale.label: Vigorous Activity
rpeScale.note: On the verge of uncomfortable, short of breath, can speak a sentence

rpeScale.label: Very Hard Activity
rpeScale.note: Hard to maintain intensity, hard to speak more than a word

rpeScale.label: Max Effort
rpeScale.note: Feels impossible to continue, completely out of breath

warmup.title: Warm-Up Prep
warmup.subtitle: Do NOT attempt any drills/runs without a proper warm-up!

steps.name: 1. Raise Heart Rate + Band Activation
steps.detail: Light jog + stretch out any tight muscle groups, then a band routine.

steps.name: 2. Activate Muscle & Mobilise Joints
steps.detail: Dynamically hit all key muscles: groins, hamstrings, glutes, quads, calves, hip flexors.

steps.name: 3. Potentiate & Prime Key Movements
steps.detail: Sharp accelerations & decelerations, cutting, single-leg plyos, changes of direction, jumps. Bursts up to 80% speed. Runs for distance 40-80m @ 70%.

preactivation.title: Pre-Activations
preactivation.subtitle: Done at the start, pre warm-up, for all running sessions.

exercises.name: Adductor Squeeze (short lever → long lever)
exercises.cue: Lie on your back and squeeze a ball or fist between the knees. Short lever = feet close to your hips; long lever = legs straight. Build to a hard squeeze and hold, breathing steadily.

exercises.name: DL Hamstring ISO
exercises.cue: Lie on your back, knees bent, heels dug into the floor. Lift your hips into a bridge and hold — you should feel the hamstrings switch on. Lower with control between reps.

exercises.name: Standing Sprinter CR (bent → straight knee, floating heel)
exercises.cue: Stand tall in a sprint posture. Drive one knee up high, then slowly straighten that knee out in front with the heel floating just off the floor. Controlled — it builds hamstring and hip control.

exercises.name: DL Wall Sits
exercises.cue: Back flat against a wall, slide down until your thighs are near parallel to the floor, knees over ankles. Hold strong and keep breathing.

exercises.name: Banded Walks (lateral)
exercises.cue: Band around your legs, athletic half-squat. Step sideways keeping constant tension on the band and your feet pointing forward — don't let the knees collapse inwards.

exercises.name: Hip Flexion (band around toes)
exercises.cue: Loop the band around your toes. Stand tall and drive the knee up against the band as high as you can, hold, then lower slowly. Stay upright — no leaning back.

exercises.name: Banded Hip Abduction (standing clamshell)
exercises.cue: Stand tall with a band above the knees. Keep hips level and rotate/open the working knee outwards against the band, hold, then control it back.

exercises.name: Hip CARs (standing)
exercises.cue: Stand tall and braced. Draw the biggest, slowest circle you can with one knee — up, out to the side, around and down — while keeping your trunk completely still.

mobility.title: Mobility Routine
mobility.subtitle: Complete before gym sessions and on recovery days.

exercises.name: Knee Hugs
exercises.cue: Standing or walking tall, pull one knee up into your chest and squeeze, balancing on the other leg. Hold briefly, lower, and swap. Keeps the glutes and hip flexors moving.

exercises.name: Straight Leg Raise (banded preferably)
exercises.cue: Lie on your back with the leg straight and toes pulled up. Raise it as high as you can with control, then lower slowly. The band adds resistance — keep the knee locked out.

exercises.name: Max Hip Flexion + Knee Extension (banded preferably)
exercises.cue: Drive the knee up high towards your chest, then straighten that knee out in front to feel the hamstring lengthen. Active and controlled — don't bounce.

exercises.name: Lumbar Rotations, Straight Leg (banded preferably)
exercises.cue: On your back, take one straight leg across your body and let the lower back rotate, keeping both shoulders pinned to the floor. Feel the stretch, then return with control.

exercises.name: Lying Pigeon Stretch
exercises.cue: On your back, cross one ankle over the opposite knee to make a figure-4, then draw both legs in towards your chest. You'll feel it in the glute/hip of the crossed leg.

exercises.name: Hip 90/90s
exercises.cue: Sit with one leg bent 90° in front and the other bent 90° to the side. Keeping your chest tall, rotate the hips to switch both legs to the other side.

exercises.name: Half-Kneeling Flexor – Hamstring Stretch
exercises.cue: From a half-kneeling lunge, shift your hips forward to stretch the back-leg hip flexor, then rock back over a straightened front leg to stretch that hamstring. Flow between the two.

exercises.name: Hip CARs
exercises.cue: On all fours (or standing), draw the biggest slow circle you can with one knee — flex it up, take it out to the side, around the back and down — keeping the rest of your body still.

nutrition.title: Fuelling & Food

examples: Bagel or toast with peanut butter
Bowl of pasta or rice with chicken
Banana or a cereal/Rice Krispie bar (if short on time)
Small handful of Haribo / jelly sweets
Glass of milk or a protein shake
Scrambled or boiled eggs on toast
Pasta or rice with mince/chicken & veg
Cheese toastie + a glass of milk

tips: You're 15 and still growing — eat enough across the day. Aim for 3 meals + 2–3 snacks; don't skip meals, especially breakfast.
Build each plate: a palm of protein (chicken, fish, eggs, mince, beans), 1–2 handfuls of carbs (potatoes, pasta, rice, bread), and half a plate of veg/fruit.
Good cheap snacks: bananas, apples, oatcakes, yoghurt, milk, cheese, malt loaf, peanut butter on toast, a handful of nuts.
Hydrate: a glass of water with every meal and a bottle to sip in training. Your pee should be pale straw — dark means drink more.
Recovery in a hurry: milk + a banana, or yoghurt with fruit, then a proper meal soon after.
Go easy on energy drinks (Monster/Prime energy) — too much caffeine for a 15-year-old. Water, milk and diluting juice do the job.
On hot days train in the cooler morning and drink extra fluids.
nutrition.note: General guidance to support training and growth for a 15-year-old — not a strict diet. For anything specific, check with the club's staff.

substitutes.title: Exercise Substitutes
substitutes.subtitle: ONLY as a last resort when an exercise can't be done due to lack of equipment.

items.alt: Elevated DL Hamstring Hold on Box 3 x 15s
Slow Tempo Press Ups (2s on the way down)
Heavy DB Row OR Lat Pulldown
Leg Press Machine OR DB Goblet Squat

benchmark5k.title: Benchmark 5km Time Trial
benchmark5k.summary: Timed 5km as fast as possible, no stops.
benchmark5k.detail: Warm up (10-15 mins). Then a timed Strava 5km as fast as possible — no stops. Pace yourself!

run1a.title: Pitch/Run 1a — Straight-Line Sprints
run1a.summary: All 100% efforts, min 1 min rest after each sprint.
run1a.detail:
Warm up (10-15 mins). Straight-line sprint work, all 100% efforts, minimum 1 min rest after each sprint:
• 2 x 20m
• 2 x 30m
• 2 x 40m
• 2 x 60m

run1b.title: Pitch/Run 1b — Intervals
run1b.detail:
Pitch perimeters @ 75% effort (fast but comfortable). The 30s run should be a faster pace than the 60s.

1 set = 60s run, 45s off, 30s run, 30s off, 60s run — then REST.
Repeat for 6 sets.

Can be done on a treadmill / track / street run.

run2.title: Pitch/Run 2 — Penalty-Box Run
run2.summary: 6-yard, pen spot & full width. Fast pace. (Can be done with a ball.)
run2.detail: Penalty width box run (can be done with a ball). 1 rep = 6-yard back to line → pen spot back to line → full width. 30s rest between sets. Each rep at fast pace.

run3a.title: Pitch/Run 3a — Slalom + Curved Sprints
run3a.detail:
Warm up (10-15 mins). All 100% efforts.
• Slalom sprint work: x8 reps, 1 min rest after each effort.
• REST 4-5 mins.
• Curved sprint work (around 1/2 the centre circle): x4 reps each side (8 in total).

run3b.title: Pitch/Run 3b — Saltire Run
run3b.summary: Diagonal runs @ 80% pace, light jog recovery.
run3b.detail: Saltire run. 1 rep = diagonal run + recovery + diagonal run + recovery. Run @ 80% pace, light jog as the rest.

run4.title: Pitch/Run 4 — Every Line and Back
run4.detail:
'Every Line and Back' — for time.
5 sets x 3 mins continuous OR 4 sets x 4 mins continuous.
2-3 mins rest between sets.

altRun1.title: Alternative Run 1 — 4 x 1km
altRun1.detail: 4 x 1km run, aiming for 3:50-4:10 per km. 2 mins rest between each 1km. Aim for consistent timings each km.

altRun2.title: Alternative Run 2 — 2 x (5 x 400m)
altRun2.detail: 2 sets of 5 x 400m. 1 set = 5 x 400m with 60s rest between efforts, then 4 mins rest between Set 1 and Set 2. Aim for consistent timings each 400m.

lowers1.title: Gym 1 — Lowers 1
lowers1.footer: Then perform 2-3 upper-body exercises of choice.

exercises.name: DB Goblet Lateral Lunge
exercises.cue: Hold a dumbbell at your chest. Step wide to one side and sit into that hip, keeping the other leg straight, then push back to the middle. Chest up.

exercises.name: DB SL RDL
exercises.cue: Single-leg Romanian deadlift. Hinge at the hip and lower the dumbbell down the standing leg, free leg reaching behind; feel the hamstring, then stand tall. Keep a flat back.

exercises.name: DB Bulgarian Split Squats
exercises.cue: Rear foot up on a bench, dumbbells by your sides. Drop straight down into the front leg with the knee over your toes, then drive back up.

exercises.name: Heel-Raised Goblet Squats
exercises.cue: Heels on a small plate or wedge, dumbbell at your chest. Squat down tall and deep with knees travelling forward, then stand.

exercises.name: Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.cue: On one foot (hold something for balance), rise up onto the ball of your foot as high as you can, pause at the top, then lower slowly.

lowers2.title: Gym 2 — Lowers 2
lowers2.footer: Then perform 2-3 upper-body exercises of choice.

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.cue: Bar on your upper back, feet shoulder-width. Brace your core and sit down and back to at least parallel, chest up, then drive up through the whole foot.

exercises.name: Single Leg Hamstring Curls (Machine)
exercises.cue: Curl the pad towards your glutes one leg at a time, squeeze, then lower slowly — control the lowering phase especially.

exercises.name: Nordics
exercises.cue: Kneel with your ankles held down. Lower your body towards the floor as slowly as you can, resisting with the hamstrings, then push off your hands to come back up.

exercises.name: Seated Knee Extensions (Machine)
exercises.cue: Straighten the knees to lift the pad, squeeze the quads at the top for a second, then lower under control.

exercises.name: Hip Flexion with Cable
exercises.cue: Cable or band on the ankle. Stand tall and drive the knee up to hip height against the resistance, then control it back down.

exercises.name: Wall Sit
exercises.cue: Back flat on the wall, thighs parallel to the floor, knees over ankles. Hold strong and keep breathing.

exercises.name: DB Walking Lunges
exercises.cue: Dumbbell in each hand, step forward into a lunge so both knees bend to about 90°, then step through into the next. Stay tall and controlled.

exercises.name: Box Jumps
exercises.cue: Quick dip, then explode up onto the box and land soft and quiet with bent knees. Step back down each rep — don't jump down.

exercises.name: SL Double Broad Jump
exercises.cue: From one leg, do two consecutive broad jumps forward, sticking and holding each landing for a second. Balanced, soft landings.

exercises.name: DB Split CMJ (continuous)
exercises.cue: In a split stance holding light dumbbells, dip and jump continuously, springing fast off the floor with soft landings each rep.

exercises.name: Rotational Med Ball Toss
exercises.cue: Stand side-on to a wall, rotate from the hips and throw the med ball hard into the wall, catch and repeat. Power comes from the trunk, not the arms.

exercises.name: Press Ups
exercises.cue: Hands under shoulders, body in a straight line. Lower the chest to the floor and push back up; keep the core tight so the hips don't sag.

exercises.name: DB Lateral Shoulder Raise
exercises.cue: Slight bend in the elbows, raise the dumbbells out to the sides up to shoulder height, then lower slowly. No swinging.

exercises.name: DB Bicep Curls
exercises.cue: Elbows tucked at your sides, curl the dumbbells up without swinging, squeeze, then lower under control.

exercises.name: DB Push Press
exercises.cue: Dumbbells at your shoulders, small dip in the knees then drive them overhead using legs and arms together. Lower back to the shoulders.

exercises.name: DB Chest Press
exercises.cue: Lying on a bench, press the dumbbells up over your chest, then lower slowly until your elbows are level with the bench.

exercises.name: Pull Ups
exercises.cue: Hang from the bar, pull your chest up towards it leading with the elbows, then lower all the way under control. Use a resistance band for help if needed.

exercises.name: DB Alternating OH Strict Press
exercises.cue: Standing tall and braced, press one dumbbell overhead at a time without leaning back, lower under control, then swap.

exercises.name: Press Ups
exercises.cue: Straight line from head to heels, lower the chest to the floor and push back up with the core tight.

exercises.name: SA Bent Over Row
exercises.cue: Single arm. Hinge forward with a flat back, row the dumbbell to your hip leading with the elbow, then lower slowly.

exercises.name: Deadbugs
exercises.cue: On your back, arms up and knees over hips. Slowly lower the opposite arm and leg towards the floor while keeping your lower back pressed flat, then switch.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.cue: On one forearm with your body in a straight line, lift the hips and hold. Don't let them drop.

weeks.title: Week 0 — Full Rest
weeks.note: FULL WEEK REST AND RECOVERY. Enjoy your break and recover physically and mentally.
